title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Acne
intro: Herbal teas for Acne are natural infusions made from various herbs, plants, and flowers that are used to treat and prevent acne breakouts. These teas are considered effective in treating acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce redness, soothe the skin, and combat the bacteria that cause acne. Some popular herbal teas used to treat acne include peppermint tea, which helps to cool and calm the skin, chamomile tea, which reduces inflammation and promotes relaxation, and green tea, which contains antioxidants that help to combat acne-causing bacteria. Other herbal teas used to treat acne include lemon balm tea, which has antiseptic properties that help to prevent infections, and licorice root tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the skin.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizomes of ginger, have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, exhibit their therapeutic potential by reducing inflammation, combating bacterial infections, and regulating the body's immune response to alleviate acne symptoms. By incorporating Zingiber officinale teas into one's skincare routine, individuals can experience benefits such as reduced redness, decreased acne severity, and improved overall skin health. The use of these herbal teas can also help to promote a balanced gut microbiome, which is essential for maintaining healthy skin and preventing acne breakouts.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of fresh ginger root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Peel the ginger root and grate it using a grater or food processor.
Steep the grated ginger in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its flavor and oils.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the ginger particles.
Let the tea cool down and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day to help reduce acne.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating acne, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely for acne, it is recommended to start with small amounts, avoid using it if you have sensitive skin, and monitor your skin condition closely for any adverse reactions, and also be aware of the potential interactions with other medications you are taking.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a week.
Start with a small dose of 1 teaspoon per cup for 2 days.
Gradually increase dose to 2 teaspoons per cup after 2 days.
Drink tea 30 minutes before meals to help digestion and acne.
Continue using tea for 4-6 weeks for best acne results.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas, derived from the gel of the aloe vera plant, have been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including acne. The anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne, promoting a faster healing process. The bioactive constituents, such as vitamins A and E, as well as aloin and aloe-emodin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacteria that contributes to acne development. The benefits of using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat acne include reduced scarring, improved skin texture, and a decrease in the appearance of acne lesions, making it a popular natural remedy for this common skin ailment.
preparation_procedure: Cut a 4-inch piece of Aloe barbadensis leaf from a healthy plant.
Wash the leaf with water to remove dirt and bacteria. Pat dry with a clean cloth.
Cut the leaf into small pieces and remove the green skin. Use only the clear gel inside.
Place 2-3 tablespoons of the clear gel into a cup and add 8 ounces of boiling water.
Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid and drink.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Infectious disorders drug targets", Aloe barbadensis teas for acne is not specifically mentioned in the provided study, but the study does mention Aloe as one of the therapeutically active natural herbal resources that are potent and safe in the treatment of dermatological infections, suggesting its potential use in managing acne.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be beneficial in treating acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues in some individuals. To use Aloe barbadensis teas safely, it is essential to start with small amounts, gradually increase the dosage, and avoid consuming it on an empty stomach to minimize potential side effects like stomach upset and diarrhea.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in cooled Aloe barbadensis tea.
Dip cloth into cooled tea, 1-2 tablespoons per cup.
Gently apply cloth to acne-affected area, 2-3 times a day.
Leave the cloth on for 15-20 minutes, or until it dries.
Repeat for 2-3 weeks, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce redness and prevent infection. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, have been found to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities that help to alleviate acne symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea purpurea teas, particularly the ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, thereby treating acne. By incorporating Echinacea purpurea teas into one's skincare routine, individuals can reap the benefits of this herbal preparation, including reduced acne severity, improved skin texture, and enhanced overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried flowers to a heat-resistant cup and pour in the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea. Mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce acne symptoms. Store any leftover tea in the fridge.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat acne, but it may also cause side effects such as mild allergic reactions, skin irritation, or an increased sensitivity to sunlight, which can lead to further skin problems. To avoid these side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to also be cautious when using Echinacea purpurea teas in conjunction with other medications or skincare products that may interact with its properties.
preparation_usage: Boil water and let it cool to 95°F to 104°F for tea.
Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2 times a day.
Use 2 to 4 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea per cup.
Steep the dried herb for 5 to 10 minutes before drinking.
Continue using the tea for 2 to 6 months for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and saponins present in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause acne. The antioxidant properties of Silybum marianum teas also help to protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, leading to a reduction in acne severity. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat acne include reduced inflammation, improved skin texture, and a decrease in the occurrence of acne lesions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Wash the leaves in cold water to remove dirt and impurities.
Combine the leaves with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool before drinking 1 cup, 2-3 times a day for acne treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used as an herbal remedy to treat acne, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and diarrhea, and allergic reactions, including skin rashes and itching. Precautions to take when using Silybum marianum teas to treat acne include monitoring blood sugar levels, as it may interact with diabetes medications, and avoiding its use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as its effects on the fetus or baby are unknown.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Silybum marianum tea for acne treatment
Start with 1 cup of tea, 2 times a day, for 2 weeks
Adjust dosage to 2 cups of tea, 2 times a day, if needed
Drink tea 30 minutes after meals and before bedtime
Monitor skin condition and adjust dosage or consult doctor if needed

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause acne. By consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to regulate skin pH, reduce sebum production, and promote wound healing, ultimately leading to a reduction in acne severity. Regular consumption of this herbal tea may also help to improve overall skin health and reduce the occurrence of acne breakouts.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for sweetness. Stir well.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature. You can store it in the fridge for later use.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a week to help reduce acne. You can also use it as a toner by soaking a cotton pad in the tea and applying it to your skin.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat acne, but possible side effects include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of photosensitivity, which may lead to sunburn or other skin damage. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small amounts and gradually increasing the dose, avoiding use during peak sun hours, and keeping the skin well moisturized to minimize potential irritation.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times daily.
Start with small dosages (1/2 tsp per cup) and adjust.
Use for 2-4 weeks for best results and then reassess.
Combine tea with a healthy lifestyle, including a balanced diet.
Monitor skin progress and adjust dosage or frequency if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce redness and prevent infection. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which aid in the treatment of acne. Curcuma longa teas help to treat acne by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, preventing the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, and promoting wound healing. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas for acne treatment include reduced inflammation, improved skin clarity, and enhanced overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root powder.
Add the measured powder to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce acne.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Archives of dermatological research", Curcuma longa teas for acne may be effective due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which involve the reduction of proinflammatory cytokines, such as IL-1ß, IL-6, IL-8, TGF-β, TNF-α, and NF-κB.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be an effective herbal preparation for treating acne, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high polyphenol and curcumin content. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, as well as to consume the tea on an empty stomach and avoid mixing it with other medications that may interact with curcumin.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Curcuma longa tea twice daily after meals.
Start with 1 teaspoon of turmeric powder per cup of tea.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons after 3 days of usage.
Continue usage for 2-3 weeks for optimal acne reduction.
Monitor your skin condition and adjust dosage as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil, have been widely used to treat acne due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including terpinen-4-ol and cineole, help to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacterium that contributes to the development of acne. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Melaleuca alternifolia teas help to soothe and clear up acne-prone skin, making it an effective natural remedy for this common skin ailment. The benefits of using Melaleuca alternifolia teas to treat acne include reduced redness, fewer breakouts, and a healthier, more balanced complexion.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot or kettle until it's hot but not boiling.
Place the dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, then remove the leaves.
Let the tea cool, then apply it to the affected skin using a cotton ball or pad.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the American journal of clinical dermatology, Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil, may have the potential to become a standard treatment for acne.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat acne, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to the sun, which may lead to sunburn or darkening of the skin. To use Melaleuca alternifolia teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small amounts and gradually increasing the dose, avoiding contact with sensitive areas, and keeping the tea away from eyes and mouth to minimize potential irritation.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of Melaleuca alternifolia tea for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and discard tea leaves, then cool to room temperature.
Wash face with gentle cleanser, then apply cooled tea as a toner.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of tea 2-3 times a day, or as needed.
Monitor skin and adjust usage as acne improves or worsens.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including acne. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as citral and geraniol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, thereby treating acne. The properties of Cymbopogon citratus teas, including its antiseptic and astringent properties, help to dry out pimples and reduce the production of sebum, which can clog pores and cause acne. The benefits of using Cymbopogon citratus teas to treat acne include reducing inflammation, preventing bacterial growth, and promoting skin clarity, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems, or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the leaves in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the leaves to release their oils and flavor.
Strain the tea into another cup to remove the leaves, and let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, after washing and toning your face, to help reduce acne.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat acne, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions when using Cymbopogon citratus teas to treat acne include using them in moderation, avoiding excessive consumption, and monitoring for signs of intolerance or sensitivity, such as skin rashes or digestive problems.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Cymbopogon citratus tea twice a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and then strain the tea.
Drink the tea for 7-10 days for noticeable improvement.
Repeat the process for 3-4 months for long-term results.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce redness and fight off bacterial infections that can cause breakouts. The flavonoids, terpenoids, and carotenoids present in Calendula officinalis teas possess strong antioxidant and photoprotective properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacterial infections, Calendula officinalis teas can help to clear up acne lesions and prevent future breakouts, promoting a clearer and more even-toned complexion. The benefits of using Calendula officinalis teas to treat acne include reduced inflammation, improved skin tone, and a decreased risk of scarring, making it a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried flowers to a heat-resistant cup or tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the dried flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the solids.
Let the tea cool down and use it as a face wash or apply it to acne-prone areas with a cotton pad.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Calendula officinalis teas for acne have a relative dermatologic importance of 57.78, indicating its potential use in treating acne based on traditional Eastern European medicinal practices.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating acne, but may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or increased sensitivity to sunlight. To use Calendula officinalis teas safely, take precautions by starting with small amounts, avoiding eye contact, and monitoring skin reactions closely, as well as using protective measures when spending time outdoors.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Calendula officinalis tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Apply the cooled tea to the affected skin using a cotton pad 2-3 times daily.
Use 1 cup of tea as a face mask and leave it on for 10-15 minutes, 1-2 times a week.
Drink 1 cup of Calendula officinalis tea daily as a supplement to promote skin health.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce redness and prevent infection. The bioactive constituents of thyme tea, including thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties, which aid in reducing the severity of acne by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause breakouts. Thyme tea's ability to balance the skin's pH and reduce inflammation also contributes to its effectiveness in treating acne. The benefits of using thyme tea to treat acne include reduced inflammation, fewer breakouts, and a healthier, more balanced skin ecosystem, making it a natural and effective alternative to traditional acne treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 5-7 fresh thyme leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried thyme.
Steep the thyme leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. If using dried thyme, steep for 2-3 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before applying it to your skin.
Soak a cotton pad or a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to your acne-prone areas for 5-10 minutes before rinsing with lukewarm water.
preparations.preparation_study: According to dermatologic therapy, Thymus vulgaris teas for acne are not mentioned in the provided study, however, Oregon grape root is mentioned as having synergistic antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and bile-stimulating properties which make the crude extract useful in acne.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used as a potential treatment for acne, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or an increase in heart rate and blood pressure due to its thymol content. To use Thymus vulgaris teas safely for acne treatment, it's essential to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid using it if you have any underlying medical conditions, such as high blood pressure or heart problems.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, also known as rosemary tea, have been used as a natural remedy to treat acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat acne by reducing redness and inflammation, controlling bacterial growth, and promoting healthy skin. The bioactive constituents of rosemary tea, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antibacterial activities that help to combat acne-causing bacteria and protect the skin from damage. Regular consumption of rosemary tea has been found to have numerous benefits, including reduced acne severity, improved skin clarity, and a reduced risk of scarring.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it sit.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for a sweeter taste.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day, ideally before bedtime, to reduce acne inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Rosmarinus officinalis teas for acne are not specifically mentioned in the provided study, however, the study highlights the use of natural products in cosmetic-containing herbals for skin issues such as acne, but it does not provide information on the effectiveness of Rosmarinus officinalis teas for this purpose.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating acne due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and stomach upset, as well as allergic reactions like skin irritation and itching. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using Rosmarinus officinalis teas during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as with individuals who have allergies or sensitivities to the plant.

Strain the tea into another cup. If desired, add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce acne. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas are being researched for their potential to treat acne, a common skin condition characterized by inflammation and bacterial overgrowth. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of ginkgo biloba teas are thought to help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also protecting the skin from oxidative damage. The bioactive constituents of ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, are believed to contribute to its anti-acne effects by inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacterium that can contribute to acne breakouts. By reducing inflammation and P. acnes growth, ginkgo biloba teas may help to improve acne symptoms and promote healthy skin.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Take 2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the tea infuser.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day for acne relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat acne, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Ginkgo biloba teas for acne treatment safely, it's essential to take them in moderation and avoid consuming them if you have any pre-existing medical conditions, such as bleeding disorders, or if you're pregnant or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress that can exacerbate acne symptoms. The herbal preparation in these teas contains bioactive constituents such as catechins, theaflavins, and thearubigins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to acne development. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting P. acnes growth, Camellia sinensis teas may help to improve acne symptoms and promote a clearer complexion. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat acne include reduced redness and inflammation, improved skin clarity, and a reduced risk of acne scarring.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Camellia sinensis leaves and add to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the cooled water over the Camellia sinensis leaves and let it steep for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce acne.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Camellia sinensis teas were not specifically mentioned as a treatment for acne in the provided study.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat acne, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to the sun due to the high levels of catechins and theaflavins present in the tea. Precautions should be taken to avoid direct skin contact with the tea, and to use a gentle cleanser to minimize skin irritation, as well as to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dosage to allow the skin to adjust.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce redness and prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate the condition. The tea's bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and terpenoids, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that help to soothe and calm the skin, promoting a reduction in acne severity. The use of Eucalyptus globulus teas can also help to balance the skin's natural pH, reducing the occurrence of acne-causing bacteria and promoting a healthy skin environment. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties can help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to acne development, making it a beneficial herbal preparation for acne treatment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh eucalyptus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Chop the eucalyptus leaves into small pieces and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the eucalyptus leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids.
Let the tea cool, then use it as a toner or apply it to your skin with a cotton pad to help reduce acne.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Eucalyptus globulus teas are not specifically mentioned in the provided study as a treatment for acne, but the study does highlight the use of natural products, including herbal products, in cosmetic-containing herbals, which may improve appearance by delivering essential nutrients to the skin, potentially alleviating conditions such as acne.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can help alleviate acne symptoms, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues, like nausea or stomach discomfort. To use Eucalyptus globulus teas safely, avoid consuming it in large quantities, especially when pregnant or breastfeeding, and monitor your skin for any adverse reactions, as its effectiveness and safety may vary from person to person.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle leaf tea, have been traditionally used to treat acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause acne. By consuming Urtica dioica teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to balance the skin's natural pH, reduce sebum production, and prevent clogged pores, ultimately leading to clearer and healthier skin. Regular consumption of Urtica dioica teas may also help to reduce the severity and frequency of acne breakouts, promoting overall skin health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Chop the Urtica dioica leaves into small pieces and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the chopped leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce acne. Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Urtica dioica teas for acne are not explicitly discussed in the provided study, however, Urtica dioica is mentioned as a plant with therapeutic properties associated with its phytoconstituents, which can be used in cosmetic-containing herbals to improve skin health and appearance.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can potentially cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues when used to treat acne, particularly if consumed in large quantities or without proper preparation. To minimize the risks, it is recommended to use Urtica dioica teas in moderation, start with small doses, and avoid consuming them if you have a history of allergies or sensitive skin conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat acne due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis teas, including quinine and alkaloids, help to reduce inflammation and combat the growth of acne-causing bacteria. By reducing inflammation and bacterial growth, Cinchona officinalis teas can help to clear acne lesions and promote healthy skin. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat acne include reduced redness, fewer breakouts, and improved overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or sugar (optional) to the tea for taste. Stir well.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature. You can store it in the fridge for later use.
Drink 1 cup of the Cinchona tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce acne symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Cinchona officinalis teas are not specifically mentioned in the given study as a treatment for acne, but the study suggests that various herbal products, including those containing natural ingredients, may have therapeutic properties that can help with skin issues such as acne.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat acne, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and an increased risk of liver damage due to its high quinine content. To use Cinchona officinalis teas safely, take caution to avoid consuming excessive amounts, as this may exacerbate the condition or lead to adverse interactions with other medications, and be mindful of its potential impact on individuals with pre-existing liver conditions or taking medications that may interact with quinine.
